title: lippia umbellata
intro: Lippia umbellata, also known as Mexican citronella or citronella, is a perennial plant native to Central and South America, characterized by its distinctive citrus scent and small white or yellow flowers. Traditionally, Lippia umbellata has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its essential oils and flavonoids. The health benefits of Lippia umbellata include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and exhibit antioxidant properties, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various health concerns. Lippia umbellata can be prepared in various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and essential oils, which can be used topically or taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
